name: tidy
description: Tidy-as-you-touch: when you edit a file, format it and surface linter findings — fixing what's safe — flag it for decomposition when it grows past a size budget, and hold a subtractive posture (reuse before create, delete what a change makes redundant) so an actively-worked project converges toward clean code and trends down in complexity over time, scoped to the file you touched. Files over budget also surface automatically at session start (a light distill) — no manual trigger. The standard goes quiet (one-line re-anchor) once it's recorded in the project's CLAUDE.md. /tidy:distill runs the whole-project weight report; /tidy:audit runs a read-only, proportional whole-project audit against the standards (tests, linters, dead code, doc gaps, currency) with a prioritized plain-language report. Edit-time linting targets Go (goimports/gofumpt + golangci-lint), web (eslint incl. jsx-a11y, stylelint — shifting Lighthouse a11y/best-practices to edit time), Python (ruff) and shell (shellcheck) — findings-only, using the project's own tool, silent otherwise; fast file-scoped tools only (slow whole-project linters stay with the verification floor). On touch it also surfaces the nearest manifest's pinned versions (package.json/go.mod/...) so the model can flag deprecated/outdated tech — never auto-upgrading. It also surfaces an approximate blast-radius of the touched file (Go via the toolchain's exact `go list` importer graph, cached per session, falling back to git grep; other languages via git grep) so a change's affected surface gets test coverage. A Stop hook is the verification floor: after Claude finishes, if the working tree changed and the project has a discoverable test command (package.json/go/pytest/cargo/Makefile, or CLAUDE_TIDY_TEST_CMD), it runs the tests and blocks until they pass (bounded to a few attempts) — the safety net a non-technical owner can't produce. Coverage ratchet: when a touched source file has no test, nudge to characterize it (pin current behavior) before changing — so under-tested/legacy projects accrue a spec on the worked surface; opt-in strict mode (CLAUDE_TIDY_COVERAGE_RATCHET=1) blocks the stop until changed source is characterized.
